query: I have an idea for a notification system that sends alerts via email, SMS, and push. can you help me think through how to build it?
what's the best way to approach adding multi-tenancy to our SaaS app? there are a few strategies and I'm not sure which fits our use case
let's brainstorm how to restructure our monolith into microservices — I want to explore different decomposition strategies before we commit
I want to build a real-time collaboration feature like Google Docs for our editor. help me design the architecture
how should we approach caching? we've got a mix of frequently-changing user data and rarely-changing product catalog data, different TTLs make sense
I have an idea for a CLI tool that generates boilerplate for our internal services. help me figure out what it should do and how it should work
think through this with me: we need an event-driven architecture for order processing but I'm not sure if we need Kafka or if SQS would be enough
I want to redesign our auth system — currently we use session cookies but we're adding a mobile app and an API for partners. what are our options?
let's design a feature flag system. should we build our own or use LaunchDarkly? what are the trade-offs for our team size?
I'm thinking about adding a recommendation engine to our e-commerce site. I need help figuring out the approach before writing any code
add a retry mechanism to the API client with exponential backoff, max 3 retries
fix the null pointer exception in UserService.getProfile when the user has no avatar
create a mermaid sequence diagram showing how the auth flow works
set up ESLint and Prettier for our TypeScript project with the airbnb config
write unit tests for the OrderService.calculateTotal method covering edge cases
create a git worktree for the auth-refactor feature branch
review the pull request and check for SQL injection vulnerabilities
set up shared skills for my new project using additionalDirectories
build a Claude Code plugin with a /deploy command
read the README and tell me how to run the development server
